idea: GatedNorm: insert learnable sigmoid gate after RMSNorm in transformer blocks (y = sigmoid(gate) * rmsnorm(x))
evidence: arxiv 2601.22966 Jan2026 'Unified View of Attention/Residual Sinks' GatedNorm mitigates sinks +2pt gain
notes: Adds n_embd params per norm site. Our norm() is called 3x per block (pre-attn, post-attn, pre-mlp) plus final. ~4*20*768=61k params. Direct fix to the sink problem + improves quantization robustness.

idea: Aggressive SWA pattern SSSSL ratio 4:1 + window 256->128 (was SSSL 3:1 256)
evidence: Qwen3.5 Feb2026 uses 5:1 ratio with 128 window. RATTENTION paper 2506.15545 'minimal window size' direction.
notes: At d20 SSSL gives 5 L-layers SSSSL gives 4 L-layers — saves compute per long-window layer. Narrower window is cheaper; may free throughput for marginal capacity. Wider window (512) was already tried and worse (81332ca).

idea: Dynamic Tanh (DyT) replacing RMSNorm: y = gamma * tanh(alpha * x) + beta with per-feature learnable alpha,gamma,beta
evidence: arxiv 2503.10622 'Transformers without Normalization' DyT matches/beats RMSNorm, +efficiency. 60 norm sites at d20 (3/block*20+final).
notes: Zero stats computation (no mean/var). Adds ~3*n_embd per site = ~138k params. May improve stability at d20 depth. Risk: gradient range different from RMSNorm; alpha init sensitive.

idea: HybridNorm: Pre-Norm on attention branch + Post-Norm on MLP branch per block (currently full Pre-Norm)
evidence: arxiv 2503.04598 HybridNorm integrates Pre+Post within each block — improved performance at depth
notes: Direct fix for PreNorm variance dilution at d20. Trivial impl: move norm from pre-mlp to post-mlp. No new params. Attention keeps pre-norm for stability.

idea: NorMuon-arxiv: row-wise neuron normalization + second-order momentum AFTER Muon orthogonalization (not stacked-tensor variant)
evidence: arxiv 2510.05491 'NorMuon: Making Muon more efficient and scalable' +11.31% training efficiency over Muon at 1.1B scale
notes: Different from normuon-04 (modded-nanogpt stacked-QKV variant). Maintains per-neuron 2nd-order stats and applies row normalization post-NS-orthogonalization. Adds second-moment buffer similar to Adam.
